__fixture__.name: start_center_restore_card_evidence_only
__fixture__.scenario: Reopen after a corrupt_restorable_state failure (checkpoint read refused). The restore card is the canonical example of why the four summary_counts must stay separable. restore_level = evidence_only is quoted verbatim from the upstream restore_prompt_record. summary_counts.evidence_only_item_count = 5 (five items retained as evidence only); exact_restore_item_count = 0 (nothing is materialised at exact); checkpoint_rollback_item_count = 0 (no checkpoint is trusted); dirty_buffer_recovery_item_count = 1 (the recovery journal still holds one dirty buffer). A flat Reopen CTA would hide the fact that five items are not restorable; this card instead renders the four counts separately and offers the three typed primary actions restore_card.restore_now (commits the single recoverable dirty buffer), restore_card.skip_once (defers the decision; retains evidence), and restore_card.open_clean (explicit open_without_restore). missing_target_states includes corrupt_restorable_state so the chip does not collapse into a generic error.

presentation_subtitle: 5 items retained as evidence · 1 dirty buffer recoverable from the journal · no checkpoint rollback applies.
